notable_pick_trades: No original 6th-round picks (traded for Jordan Mason and Sam Howell). Expecting 3 compensatory picks: 3rd (Sam Darnold), 4th (Daniel Jones), 5th (Cam Robinson). Acquired 5th from PHI (Sam Howell trade), 7th from HOU (Ed Ingram trade). Greenard trade could add a Day 2 pick.
roster_context: The Vikings are in full roster tear-down mode after going 9-8 in 2025 under an interim GM (Rob Brzezinski replaced fired Kwesi Adofo-Mensah). They're $46M+ over the $301.2M salary cap — second worst in the NFL — and are shedding expensive veterans signed by the previous regime. J.J. McCarthy's uneven 10-start season (1,632 yards, 6-4 record) leaves QB as the franchise's defining question. The defense ranked 6th in EPA/play but is losing multiple starters. Harrison Smith's likely retirement and the gutting of the DT room create urgent needs across the back seven and interior DL. Kevin O'Connell and Brian Flores return, preserving scheme continuity.

needs.explanation: Harrison Smith (age 37) is heavily expected to retire after 14 seasons — he received a full farewell sendoff in the Week 18 finale and said he's 'leaning one way.' Josh Metellus is a solid rotational piece but not a starting-caliber replacement. Jay Ward saw more work late in 2025 but hasn't proven he can start full-time. Safety was already thin — the Vikings lost Cam Bynum to Indianapolis in 2025 free agency and never adequately replaced him. If Smith retires, Minnesota has no proven starting safety on the roster and must address this in Round 1 or early Round 2.
needs.current_starter: Harrison Smith (likely retiring) / Josh Metellus (IR late 2025)
needs.depth: Jay Ward (2023 4th-rounder, limited sample), Theo Jackson. Extremely thin if Smith retires.
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1-2 — Dillon Thieneman (Oregon), Emmanuel McNeil-Warren (Toledo), Caleb Downs (Ohio State) if he slides

needs.explanation: Byron Murphy Jr. is the CB1 but was inconsistent — the Vikings had only 8 interceptions in 2025 (tied 25th). Isaiah Rodgers is signed only through 2026 with no long-term commitment. Jeff Okudah (UFA) played only 6 games in 2025 due to injuries and is not expected back. The Vikings have lacked a true shutdown corner for years, and nearly every mock draft has them selecting a CB at No. 18. Brian Flores' heavy man-coverage scheme demands elite individual cover ability that the current room lacks.
needs.depth: Very thin — Okudah is a UFA, Fabian Moreau is a depth piece. No young developmental corners on roster.
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1 — Avieon Terrell (Clemson), Brandon Cisse (South Carolina), Colton Hood (Tennessee), Mansoor Delane (LSU)

needs.explanation: The Vikings are releasing BOTH Javon Hargrave ($10.9M savings) and Jonathan Allen ($6.5M savings) after just one season. This guts the interior defensive line. Jalen Redmond was arguably the team's best interior DL in 2025 and will be retained as an ERFA, but behind him the depth is Levi Drake Rodriguez, Tyrion Ingram-Dawkins, and CFL pickup Jaylon Hutchings — none are proven NFL starters. Minnesota desperately needs a young, disruptive interior presence to pair with Redmond.
needs.depth: Levi Drake Rodriguez, Tyrion Ingram-Dawkins, Jaylon Hutchings (CFL), Elijah Williams — unproven depth
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1-2 — Caleb Banks (Florida), Kayden McDonald (Ohio State), Peter Woods (Clemson), Lee Hunter (Texas Tech)

needs.explanation: Center Ryan Kelly suffered three concussions in 2025, played only 8 games, and is expected to retire or be released (turns 33 in May). If Kelly goes, the Vikings have no proven starting center — Blake Brandel filled in admirably as a swing player in 2025 but is a free agent and not a long-term answer at center. Will Fries (RG) and Donovan Jackson (LG, 2025 1st-rounder) are solid pieces under contract, but the center position is a gaping hole. Michael Jurgens is a backup-caliber option.
needs.current_starter: Donovan Jackson (LG), Ryan Kelly (C — likely gone), Will Fries (RG)
needs.depth: Blake Brandel (UFA, swing player MVP in 2025), Michael Jurgens (backup C). Jackson and Fries are locked in at guard.
needs.draft_range_target: Round 2-4 — Connor Lew, Jake Slaughter. May also target a center in free agency (Tyler Biadasz, Cade Mays).

needs.explanation: Eric Wilson (UFA) had a breakout 2025 (115 tackles, 6.5 sacks, 4 forced fumbles) but is 31 and a free agent with interest from other teams. Ivan Pace Jr. (RFA) saw his role diminish drastically as his PFF grade cratered to 42.3 and is not expected back. Blake Cashman is the only LB under contract for 2026 and is on the final year of his deal (turns 30). If Wilson leaves, the Vikings have essentially one playable linebacker. Even if Wilson re-signs, they need youth — both starters would be 30+.
needs.depth: Eric Wilson (UFA — likely re-sign but not guaranteed), Ivan Pace Jr. (RFA — likely gone). Released Kobe King midseason.
needs.draft_range_target: Round 2-4 — CJ Allen (Georgia), Anthony Hill Jr. (Texas), Sonny Styles (Ohio State, LB/S hybrid)

needs.explanation: Aaron Jones is being released ($7.75M savings) after an injury-plagued 2025 (12 games, 548 rushing yards). Jordan Mason, acquired from San Francisco in March 2025, had a productive year (758 yards, 6 TDs on 159 carries) and projects as the RB1. However, Mason has only one year left on his deal, and the Vikings need a strong run game to support McCarthy's development. The depth behind Mason (Zavier Scott, Ty Chandler) is limited. A Day 2 running back could be a high-value addition.
needs.depth: Zavier Scott (ERFA), Ty Chandler. C.J. Ham (FB) retired.
needs.draft_range_target: Round 2-4 — Jeremiyah Love if he falls, Jadarian Price (Notre Dame), Emmett Johnson (Nebraska, local product)

needs.explanation: Justin Jefferson is locked in as an elite WR1. Jordan Addison is entering the final year of his rookie deal (5th-year option decision due May 1) and remains a strong WR2 despite the team's passing struggles. However, Jalen Nailor (UFA) has 10+ teams pursuing him and is likely priced out of Minnesota's budget. If Nailor leaves, the WR3 spot is a void — Myles Price and Tai Felton are special teamers, not reliable targets. The Vikings could draft a bigger-bodied WR to complement Jefferson and Addison.
needs.depth: Jalen Nailor (UFA, likely departing), Myles Price, Tai Felton — very thin at WR3 if Nailor leaves
needs.draft_range_target: Round 3-5 — KC Concepcion (Texas A&M), Ted Hurst (Georgia State, size), Jordyn Tyson (Arizona State) if available

needs.explanation: Christian Darrisaw (LT) is a franchise tackle but has dealt with significant injuries two years running — he started only 10 games in 2025 and never regained elite form post-knee injury. He's only 26 and under contract. Brian O'Neill (RT) is in his final contract year (turns 31 in September) — the Vikings may extend him or need a long-term plan. Justin Skule (UFA) was the swing tackle but is not a viable long-term starter. Tackle depth is thin and Darrisaw's durability is a growing concern.
needs.current_starter: Christian Darrisaw (LT, under contract), Brian O'Neill (RT, final year)
needs.depth: Justin Skule (UFA), Walter Rouse, Blake Brandel (UFA, versatile). Thin if Darrisaw misses time again.
needs.draft_range_target: Round 3-5 — developmental swing tackle for depth/insurance

needs.explanation: T.J. Hockenson is willing to take a pay cut to stay, and the Vikings are expected to restructure his deal. He was used primarily as a blocking TE in 2025 after his knee injury, but remains a quality player. Josh Oliver provides a secondary option. However, there's no young pass-catching TE on the roster for the future — Hockenson's long-term outlook post-injury is uncertain. A developmental TE in the mid-rounds would be wise.
needs.depth: Josh Oliver, Ben Sims, Ben Yurosek. Adequate but aging.
needs.draft_range_target: Round 4-6 — Eli Stowers (Vanderbilt) had a strong Combine

free_agency_impact: Releasing Aaron Jones (RB) — saves $7.75M, creates RB need behind Jordan Mason
Releasing Javon Hargrave (DT) — saves $10.9M, guts DT room alongside Allen release
Releasing Jonathan Allen (DT) — saves $6.5M, leaves Jalen Redmond as only proven interior DL
Jonathan Greenard (EDGE) being shopped — would save $12.25M cap, could net Day 2 pick. Dallas Turner (8 sacks in 2025) and Andrew Van Ginkel would become primary EDGE rushers if traded.
Harrison Smith (S) — strongly expected to retire after emotional Week 18 sendoff. Creates massive hole at safety.
Ryan Kelly (C) — suffered 3 concussions in 2025, expected to retire or be released. Creates center void.
Eric Wilson (LB) — UFA after breakout 2025. Vikings want to re-sign but cap is extremely tight. If he leaves, LB room is barren.
Jalen Nailor (WR) — UFA, 10+ teams interested, likely priced out of MIN's tight budget. WR3 becomes a need.
Ivan Pace Jr. (LB) — RFA, likely not retained after PFF grade cratered to 42.3 in 2025.
Jalen Redmond (DT) — ERFA, will be retained. Was arguably the team's best interior DL in 2025.
T.J. Hockenson (TE) — willing to take pay cut, expected to have contract restructured to stay.
Brian O'Neill (RT) — in final contract year, may receive extension to create cap savings on $23.1M due in 2026.
Kwesi Adofo-Mensah fired as GM — Rob Brzezinski serving as interim GM. New GM search postponed until after draft.
scheme_fit_notes: Brian Flores' defense is blitz-heavy and man-coverage intensive — the 4th-highest blitz rate in the NFL in 2025. This means CB prospects must be able to play press-man, not just zone. Safety prospects need range and tackling ability to work as single-high or in Flores' versatile packages. LB prospects with blitz ability and sideline-to-sideline range are ideal (Wilson's profile). DT prospects should be interior disruptors who collapse pockets quickly — Flores creates pressure through chaos, not just edge speed. Kevin O'Connell's offense is pass-first and scheme-dependent; protecting McCarthy behind a revamped OL is paramount. The draft class is deep at WR, DT, LB, S, and CB — positions where the Vikings have needs — which gives them flexibility at 18.
